sections.title: Global Position
points: It is the only continent crossed by both the Equator (0°) and the Prime Meridian (0°).
It is crossed by three major latitudes: Equator, Tropic of Cancer, and Tropic of Capricorn.
Because of these lines, Africa lies in all four hemispheres (North, South, East, West).

sections.title: Mathematical Location (Lat/Long)
points: This means the equator divides Africa almost into two equal halves.

sections.text: Note: The Prime Meridian crosses Ghana (Accra), but the International Date Line (180°) is on the opposite side of the world and does NOT cross Africa.

cards.fact: The imaginary line (0° Latitude) that divides the earth into Northern and Southern hemispheres.
The imaginary line (0° Longitude) used to calculate international time.
A piece of land surrounded by water on three sides (e.g., The Horn of Africa).
